Prácticas en Estudios y Soluciones Eléctricas




Nuestro negocio de Redes de Electricidad España (UFD) está inmerso en un apasionante reto de transformación, que tiene como misión la digitalización y sensorización de nuestra red eléctrica para evolucionar de una red meramente física a una red virtual, donde el análisis de datos y la inteligencia artificial nos permitan gestionar y dar respuesta a todas las necesidades del nuevo ecosistema sostenible.

¡Queremos ser los mejores en lo que hacemos! Trabajando en equipo, haciendo más con menos y desarrollando a cada persona, para crecer juntos tanto en el ámbito de Redes Electricidad España como en el de otros negocios.

En este contexto ofrecemos una Beca en el negocio de Redes Electricidad España.




Estas prácticas te permitirán participar en:

Generación de maestros de reclamaciones y análisis de la red en función de los inputs de calidad de suministro:

Análisis de calidad del producto: Realizar estudios detallados sobre subtensiones y sobretensiones en la red de baja tensión, asociadas a reclamaciones de clientes de UFD. Al analizar estas reclamaciones, se ha observado que las soluciones aplicadas para resolver los problemas se pueden clasificar en distintos tipos. Para cada tipo se seleccionará una solución representativa, que estará respaldada por un análisis y una justificación detallada, explicando el motivo de la elección y cómo se aplicará en cada caso.

Representación y análisis de datos: Utilizar programas de gestión de datos para procesar y filtrar grandes volúmenes de datos obtenidos de los diferentes eventos registrados, aplicando los criterios aprendidos durante las prácticas y aquellos empleados en escenarios reales. Además, emplear Power BI como herramienta para la representación gráfica de las anomalías en la red, facilitando la interpretación de los resultados y la toma de decisiones de manera más práctica y eficiente.
Gestion y agrupación de datos: Elaborar el análisis e ingesta de datos de manera agrupada.



Lo que necesitas:

Ser estudiante del último curso de Grado o Máster en Ingeniería Eléctrica.

Conocimientos de redes eléctricas en baja y media tensión y los distintos elementos que componen el sistema eléctrico

Ayuda al estudio

Prácticas en Estudios y Soluciones Eléctricas


Nuestro negocio de Redes de Electricidad España (UFD) está inmerso en un apasionante reto de transformación, que tiene como misión la digitalización y sensorización de nuestra red eléctrica para evolucionar de una red meramente física a una red virtual, donde el análisis de datos y la inteligencia artificial nos permitan gestionar y dar respuesta a todas las necesidades del nuevo ecosistema sostenible.
¡Queremos ser los mejores en lo que hacemos! Trabajando en equipo, haciendo más con menos y desarrollando a cada persona, para crecer juntos tanto en el ámbito de Redes Electricidad España como en el de otros negocios.
En este contexto ofrecemos una Beca en el negocio de Redes Electricidad España.


Estas prácticas te permitirán participar en:
Generación de maestros de reclamaciones y análisis de la red en función de los inputs de calidad de suministro:
Análisis de calidad del producto: Realizar estudios detallados sobre subtensiones y sobretensiones en la red de baja tensión, asociadas a reclamaciones de clientes de UFD. Al analizar estas reclamaciones, se ha observado que las soluciones aplicadas para resolver los problemas se pueden clasificar en distintos tipos. Para cada tipo se seleccionará una solución representativa, que estará respaldada por un análisis y una justificación detallada, explicando el motivo de la elección y cómo se aplicará en cada caso.
Representación y análisis de datos: Utilizar programas de gestión de datos para procesar y filtrar grandes volúmenes de datos obtenidos de los diferentes eventos registrados, aplicando los criterios aprendidos durante las prácticas y aquellos empleados en escenarios reales. Además, emplear Power BI como herramienta para la representación gráfica de las anomalías en la red, facilitando la interpretación de los resultados y la toma de decisiones de manera más práctica y eficiente.
Gestion y agrupación de datos: Elaborar el análisis e ingesta de datos de manera agrupada.

Ser estudiante del último curso de Grado o Máster en Ingeniería Eléctrica.
Conocimientos de redes eléctricas en baja y media tensión y los distintos elementos que componen el sistema eléctrico

Who We Are

Boston Consulting Group partners with leaders in business and society to tackle their most important challenges and capture their greatest opportunities. BCG was the pioneer in business strategy when it was founded in 1963. Today, we help clients with total transformation-inspiring complex change, enabling organizations to grow, building competitive advantage, and driving bottom-line impact.

To succeed, organizations must blend digital and human capabilities. Our diverse, global teams bring deep industry and functional expertise and a range of perspectives to spark change. BCG delivers solutions through leading-edge management consulting along with technology and design, corporate and digital ventures—and business purpose. We work in a uniquely collaborative model across the firm and throughout all levels of the client organization, generating results that allow our clients to thrive.


We Are BCG X


We’re a diverse team of more than 3,000 tech experts united by a drive to make a difference. Working across industries and disciplines, we combine our experience and expertise to tackle the biggest challenges faced by society today. We go beyond what was once thought possible, creating new and innovative solutions to the world’s most complex problems. Leveraging BCG’s global network and partnerships with leading organizations, BCG X provides a stable ecosystem for talent to build game-changing businesses, products, and services from the ground up, all while growing their career. Together, we strive to create solutions that will positively impact the lives of millions.


What You'll Do

Our BCG X teams own the full analytics value-chain end to end: framing new business challenges, designing innovative algorithms, implementing, and deploying scalable solutions, and enabling colleagues and clients to fully embrace AI. Our product offerings span from fully custom-builds to industry specific leading edge AI software solutions.
You'll be part of our rapidly growing team who will have a chance to apply data science methods and analytics to real-world business situations across a variety of industries to drive significant business impact. You'll have the chance to partner with clients in a variety of BCG regions and industries, and on key topics like climate change, enabling them to design, build, and deploy new and innovative solutions.
Additional responsibilities will include developing and delivering thought leadership in scientific communities and papers as well as leading conferences on behalf of BCG X. Successful candidates are intellectually curious builders who are biased toward action, creative, and communicative.
We are looking for dedicated individuals with a passion for data science, statistics, operations research and redefining organizations into AI led innovative companies. Successful candidates possess the following:
Comfortable in a client-facing role with the ambition to lead teams
Likes to distill complex results or processes into simple, clear visualizations
Explain sophisticated data science concepts in an understandable manner
Love building things and are comfortable working with modern development tools and writing code collaboratively (bonus points if you have a software development or DevOps experience)
Significant experience applying advanced analytics to business situations and a proven ability to synthesize complex data
Deep understanding of modern machine learning techniques and their mathematical underpinnings, and can translate this into business implications for our clients
Have strong project management skills

Descripcion_Completa:
Locations: Madrid | Barcelona

Who We Are

Boston Consulting Group partners with leaders in business and society to tackle their most important challenges and capture their greatest opportunities. BCG was the pioneer in business strategy when it was founded in 1963. Today, we help clients with total transformation-inspiring complex change, enabling organizations to grow, building competitive advantage, and driving bottom-line impact.

To succeed, organizations must blend digital and human capabilities. Our diverse, global teams bring deep industry and functional expertise and a range of perspectives to spark change. BCG delivers solutions through leading-edge management consulting along with technology and design, corporate and digital ventures—and business purpose. We work in a uniquely collaborative model across the firm and throughout all levels of the client organization, generating results that allow our clients to thrive.


We Are BCG X


We’re a diverse team of more than 3,000 tech experts united by a drive to make a difference. Working across industries and disciplines, we combine our experience and expertise to tackle the biggest challenges faced by society today. We go beyond what was once thought possible, creating new and innovative solutions to the world’s most complex problems. Leveraging BCG’s global network and partnerships with leading organizations, BCG X provides a stable ecosystem for talent to build game-changing businesses, products, and services from the ground up, all while growing their career. Together, we strive to create solutions that will positively impact the lives of millions.


What You'll Do

Our BCG X teams own the full analytics value-chain end to end: framing new business challenges, designing innovative algorithms, implementing, and deploying scalable solutions, and enabling colleagues and clients to fully embrace AI. Our product offerings span from fully custom-builds to industry specific leading edge AI software solutions.
You’ll be part of our rapidly growing engineering team and help to build the next generation of AI solutions. You’ll have the chance to partner with clients in a variety of BCG regions and industries, and on key topics like climate change, enabling them to design, build, and deploy new and innovative solutions. Additional responsibilities will include developing and delivering thought leadership in scientific communities and papers as well as leading conferences on behalf of BCG X.
We are looking for dedicated individuals with a passion for software development, large-scale data analytics and redefining organizations into AI led innovative companies. Successful candidates possess the following:
Apply software development practices and standards to develop robust and maintainable software
Actively involved in every part of the software development life cycle
Experienced at guiding non-technical teams and consultants in and best practices for robust software development
Optimize and enhance computational efficiency of algorithms and software design
Motivated by a fast-paced, service-oriented environment and interacting directly with clients on new features for future product releases
Enjoy collaborating in teams to share software design and solution ideas
A natural problem-solver and intellectually curious across a breadth of industries and topics

Titulo: Expert Consultant, Supply Chain, Operations, Spain
Descripcion_Completa:
Locations: Madrid | Barcelona

Who We Are

Boston Consulting Group partners with leaders in business and society to tackle their most important challenges and capture their greatest opportunities. BCG was the pioneer in business strategy when it was founded in 1963. Today, we help clients with total transformation-inspiring complex change, enabling organizations to grow, building competitive advantage, and driving bottom-line impact.

To succeed, organizations must blend digital and human capabilities. Our diverse, global teams bring deep industry and functional expertise and a range of perspectives to spark change. BCG delivers solutions through leading-edge management consulting along with technology and design, corporate and digital ventures—and business purpose. We work in a uniquely collaborative model across the firm and throughout all levels of the client organization, generating results that allow our clients to thrive.


Practice Area


The Operations Practice (OPS) Area is one of BCG's largest functional practices. We help our clients create competitive advantage by enhancing their performance and building strategic capabilities. BCG’s expertise in Operations covers the following areas - supply chain management, procurement, manufacturing, service operations, cost efficiency, operational transformation, research & product development, program and project management, all with a strong digital and analytics overlay.


What You'll Do

As an Expert Consultant, you will be part of a BCG team and you will leverage your deep expertise on projects that reshape businesses. You will be given end-to-end responsibility for a workstream within a BCG project. You will work closely with clients to understand their issues, create strategies for change, and win buy-in for your recommendations, while also collaborating with colleagues on complex client issues. You will provide deep expertise and insight to clients and help build your expertise area within BCG. A majority of your time will be focused on supporting and executing case work.


The Expert Consulting Track (ECT) is vital to BCG’s ability to successfully meet our clients’ demand for deep expertise and advanced technical capabilities. Experts deliver next-level impact for BCG's most complex and cutting-edge client challenges. Experts focus on developing BCG's thought leadership, commercial capabilities, and intellectual property assets; and their work is essential to our delivery model. Working alongside our traditional and specialty consultants to bring the ‘best of BCG’ to our clients, Experts come from diverse backgrounds, with expertise often gained outside of BCG in industry, academia, or specialized consulting. Consulting work is varied and rigorous, much of it performed at our client sites. Projects can vary in length, size, and location, depending on the client’s challenge. Because our clients operate all over the world, you may travel internationally.


What You'll Bring

At least 5 years of work experience in top-tier consulting firms or leading industry companies
Strong expertise in Supply Chain and Logistics topics, such as: digital supply chain, network optimization, inventory optimization, warehouse optimization and E2E logistics transformation
Strong consulting skill set with a demonstrated willingness to learn and grow: creative and hypothesis-led problem solving, framing and communicating complex ideas, thinking strategically and excellent numerical skills
Entrepreneurial, driven, and proactive mindset with a strong work ethic
Collaborative working style with a proven ability to team effectively across BCG and with clients
Ability to build trust and rapport with clients by deeply understanding their business and challenges
Strong educational background. MBA is a plus
Fluent in Spanish and English

Who We Are

Boston Consulting Group partners with leaders in business and society to tackle their most important challenges and capture their greatest opportunities. BCG was the pioneer in business strategy when it was founded in 1963. Today, we help clients with total transformation-inspiring complex change, enabling organizations to grow, building competitive advantage, and driving bottom-line impact.

To succeed, organizations must blend digital and human capabilities. Our diverse, global teams bring deep industry and functional expertise and a range of perspectives to spark change. BCG delivers solutions through leading-edge management consulting along with technology and design, corporate and digital ventures—and business purpose. We work in a uniquely collaborative model across the firm and throughout all levels of the client organization, generating results that allow our clients to thrive.


We Are BCG X


We’re a diverse team of more than 3,000 tech experts united by a drive to make a difference. Working across industries and disciplines, we combine our experience and expertise to tackle the biggest challenges faced by society today. We go beyond what was once thought possible, creating new and innovative solutions to the world’s most complex problems. Leveraging BCG’s global network and partnerships with leading organizations, BCG X provides a stable ecosystem for talent to build game-changing businesses, products, and services from the ground up, all while growing their career. Together, we strive to create solutions that will positively impact the lives of millions.


What You'll Do

BCG X is BCG’s premier data science and advanced analytics capability with 1000+ data scientists, engineers, and analysts globally. Within BCG X, our X.Delivery Teams are our hybrid client-facing/consulting-support that leverages available tools and code bases to deliver powerful and specialized insights that help our clients tackle their most pressing business problems. We partner with BCG case teams across the full analytics value chain: framing new business challenges and problems, designing and executing innovative analytical solutions, and interpreting findings for our clients. GeoAnalytics is a sub-team in X.Delivery with expertise in geospatial data, analytics and technology. Our 35+ member team is a global resource, working with clients in every BCG region and in every industry area. It is a core member of BCG X - a rapidly growing analytics enterprise at BCG deploying a constellation of teams focused on driving practical results for BCG clients by applying leading edge technologies.


As an Analyst in GeoAnalytics, you will be solving a variety of location-based analytical problems for our clients. You will utilize your knowledge of geospatial data and analytics while using tools such as Geographic Information Systems (GIS), Python, and visualization/analytical platforms (ex. Tableau, Alteryx). You will leverage a range of techniques such as geospatial clustering, network analysis, location-based optimization and predictive analytics/modeling. You will be expected to distill insights from your analysis and link them to business objectives. You will contribute to IP development, content development, and support internal marketing.


You will also have opportunities to experiment with emerging capabilities, including the selective use of GenAI to enhance analysis workflows and accelerate insight generation.


What You'll Bring

Education & Experience Qualifications:

1-3 years of relevant work experience in GeoAnalytics with advanced degree preferred
Relevant degree in geospatial related field such as: Geography, Geology, Environmental Science, Remote Sensing, Urban Planning, Statistics/GeoStatistics, Applied Mathematics, or Computer Science
Previous experience working in a global organization or professional services firm is preferred


Demonstrated experience in the following:

Conducting web-based research for open data sources; handling, understanding, and assessing new datasets; and stitching together disparate datasets to conduct custom analyses
Applying geospatial data, tools, and technology to solve problems for businesses across multiple topics
Technical experience in the following:
GIS software such as ESRI ArcGIS or QGIS
Data management skills (e.g. data modeling, QA/QC, querying, analysis)
Data science programming/coding in Python and/or R including use of spatial libraries/packages (eg GeoPandas, Rasterio, GDAL/OGR, Terra..)
Business Intelligence/data visualization platforms such as Tableau, Power BI, etc.
Applied descriptive statistics and analytical methods (e.g., correlation, regression, clustering) within machine learning frameworks to address geospatial problems
Remote sensing skills (handling, processing, and deriving insights) and optimization skills (linear programming/heuristics) are beneficial
Web-GIS development skills (JavaScript, HTML, CSS) in ArcGIS environment (JS SDK, Python API, ExB custom widgets, etc.) are beneficial
Familiarity with optimization models applied to location-based problems (VRP, Network Optimization, Supply Chain Optimization, etc.) is beneficial

Descripcion_Completa:
Work & Study Program: Hotel Management Degree
Descripción de la empresa

Our heart beats turquoise.

Working at Motel One is like coming home to friends, where you can be your true self and where appreciation and support matter. We meet on an equal footing and see ourselves as one team: from our management to our trainees.

We create a home away from home for our guests with our shared passion for modern design and high-quality service. Our more than 100 hotels in 14 countries impress with exclusive design, high service standards and first-class inner-city locations at an attractive price.

As the founder and pioneer of the budget design concept, we are successfully expanding in the metropolises of Europe and beyond. Become part of our team and develop Motel One with us.

Descripción del empleo

Where theory meets practice – and you’re right there making it happen.

You love making people feel welcome – while at the same time you want to study, work in a hotel, and take on responsibility? Then our Work & Study program in Hotel Management is exactly right for you: you combine academic knowledge with real hands-on hotel experience and develop step by step into a hospitality professional.

Practical part

During the practical phases, you’re not just “along for the ride” – you’re fully involved. You get to know different departments of the hotel and understand how everything works together. You will work in a part-time position (approx. 20-25h/week) with appropriate compensation at a European Motel One hotel you are applying for.

Front Office & Reservations: check-in, guest communication, service quality
Breakfast & Bar: processes, standards, and service organization
Housekeeping & Quality Checks: cleanliness and cooperation with external partners
Revenue & Sales: pricing strategies and customer acquisition
Back Office & Administration: purchasing, figures, and reports

With us, you don’t just learn theory – you apply it directly. From day one, you’re part of the team, taking responsibility for your tasks and your own projects. In addition, you have the opportunity to complete a four-week international internship at one of our hotels across Europe.

Your virtual studies

During your studies, you build the foundation for a career in hospitality. Typical subjects include, for example: business administration and hotel & HR management, marketing, IT & AI in hospitality industry.

Internationally renowned and state-recognised Bachelor of Arts degree in Hospitality Management (currently in the accreditation process)
Duration: 7 semester
Access to online courses, video materials and books
The unique advantage: Motel One covers the tuition fees
Workshops at the Motel One Campus in Munich for personal exchange with other students
Opportunity to improve your language skills through Babbel for Business
Benefits:
Bonus program: The better the guests rate their stay, the higher the bonus!
Make an international career: Discover a variety of career opportunities in our hotels nationally and internationally, or join one of our numerous new openings.
Staff discounts: Benefit from attractive staff rates in all of our hotels throughout Europe and the USA.
Develop personally and professionally: With an extensive onboarding, annual trainings and coaching at our One University, you will hit the ground running.
Celebrate together: Whether it's a summer party, holiday party or our annual international soccer tournament - we celebrate successes together with the team.
Requisitos

What really counts? Your passion for hospitality!

University entrance qualification (= A-levels / technical baccalaureate or vocational training certificate incl. proof of three years of full-time professional experience)
Strong English proficiency (The working language in the hotel is the respective local language whereas the virtual studies are conducted in English.)
High degree of self-discipline, ability to work in a team, and resilience
Positive demeanour, commitment, and pleasure in contact with guests
Initial experience in the hotel industry or willingness to do a pre-study internship at Motel One

What we offer:

Study + salary: We cover the tuition fees at ISM as well as travel and accommodation costs in Munich. In addition to a monthly allowance, you will participate in our internal bonus scheme and enjoy a wide range of benefits.
Structured onboarding with dedicated contacts and support
A workplace with real prospects: Motel One is international, dynamic, and full of opportunities – with a retention rate of 70–80% for our Work & Study graduates.

You can find more information on our careers page.
Interested? We look forward to getting to know you!

Based in our Madrid office, the Head of Client Services & Traffic leads the Iberia Client Services & Traffic team and acts as the senior point of contact for key clients, broadcasters & publishers and internal stakeholders. The role is responsible for securing high quality delivery across Spain and Portugal, managing and developing the team, and supporting the Managing & Sales Director by taking full ownership of day to day client service leadership and complex client situations.

This position is key to protecting revenue, driving retention and work closely with the Managing & Sales Director to maximize growth across the client portfolio and acts as a key interface with international clients and global XR teams, ensuring smooth coordination across functions and markets.

Monitor and report on team workload, service levels and client satisfaction, using data and client feedback to identify risks, opportunities and areas for improvement.

Foster a culture of collaboration between Client Services &Traffic, Technical Operations and Billing to secure an efficient end to end delivery process.

Significant experience in client service and operations in media, advertising or a related industry.
Proven track record leading teams in a fast paced, service driven environment.
Strong client facing skills, able to build credibility with senior stakeholders at agencies, advertisers and broadcasters & publishers.
Clear commercial acumen, comfortable supporting renewals, negotiations and identifying growth opportunities.
Excellent communication, prioritisation and problem solving skills, with a hands on and proactive approach.
High level of resilience and ability to manage multiple stakeholders and deadlines.
Fluent in Spanish, Portuguese and professional level English.

LOCATION
International College Spain, Madrid

JOB TITLE
School Counsellor

JOB PURPOSE

The Secondary Counsellor supports the social, emotional and academic wellbeing of students in the Secondary School. Working as part of the Counselling Team, the post-holder provides professional counselling services, contributes to whole-school wellbeing initiatives, and works closely with students, families and staff to ensure that all students are able to thrive and fully access the educational programme at International College Spain.

REPORTING TO
Head Counsellor and Designated Safeguarding Lead (DSL) and works in close collaboration with the Secondary Leadership Team.

OTHER KEY RELATIONSHIPS

Counselling Team, Grade Level Leaders, Students, parents/guardians, Teachers, Learning Support Team,.

REMUNERATION
Competitive

KEY RESULT AREA

Counselling and Student Support

· Provide individual counselling to students who are self-referred or referred by pastoral staff or safeguarding leads.

· Support students’ emotional wellbeing, self-awareness and resilience, enabling them to manage challenges that may affect their learning or personal development.

· Draw on a range of counselling approaches appropriate to the needs of individual students.

Crisis and Risk Management

· Provide counselling support in response to critical incidents or crises affecting individuals or the wider school community.

· Contribute to the development and implementation of risk assessments and support plans for students requiring higher levels of intervention.

· Work collaboratively with internal and external professionals where appropriate.

· Balance student confidentiality with safeguarding and parental communication responsibilities in line with school policy.

Referral and Liaison

· Refer students to external specialists (e.g. psychologists, psychiatrists) when concerns indicate a risk to wellbeing, in consultation with the DSL, school leadership and families.

· Liaise closely with parents/guardians, pastoral leaders and teachers to ensure a coordinated and supportive approach.

Whole-School Wellbeing

· Actively contribute to the promotion of wellbeing across the Secondary School community.

· Support the delivery and development of the ICS Wellbeing Curriculum, wellbeing-related initiatives, workshops and learning opportunities for students, staff and parents.

· Contribute to the embedding of positive wellbeing practices within school life.

· Use wellbeing data to identify trends and inform preventative interventions within the Secondary School.

Consultation and Collaboration

· Consult with staff who refer students to gain a shared understanding of student needs and appropriate strategies.

· Participate in case conferences and multi-disciplinary meetings for students requiring additional support.

· Share relevant information professionally and confidentially to support student wellbeing and safeguarding.

Organizational and Staff Support

· Offer consultation, guidance and appropriate support to colleagues to promote staff wellbeing and a positive working culture.

Record Keeping and Professional Practice

· Maintain accurate, confidential and well-organised records in line with professional and school expectations.

· Adhere to ethical guidelines and professional standards of practice at all times.

Transition and Student Care

· Support students and families during key transitions, including entry to Secondary School, movement between year groups and preparation for post-secondary pathways.

· Provide targeted transition support for vulnerable or at-risk students.

Professional Development

· Engage in ongoing professional learning and reflective practice.

· Participate in appraisal, supervision and professional development processes.

- Contribute positively to the wider life and professional culture of International College Spain.

PERSON SPECIFICATIONS

Qualifications / Training

· A postgraduate qualification in Adolescent Counselling, Psychology or a related field.

· Relevant professional accreditation (e.g. BACP or equivalent).

· Experience of working with adolescents in a school setting.

· Strong interpersonal skills and the ability to work collaboratively within a team.

· A clear commitment to safeguarding and student wellbeing.

Other Requirements

- Compliance with safeguarding, visa and employment requirements relevant to working in Spain.

- A willingness to contribute to the ethos, values and community life of International College Spain.

Personal Attributes

- Self-motivated, flexible and adaptable to different tasks at hand

- Proactive and calm working under pressure

- A strong ability to empathise with others

- Has gravitas that allows them to be accepted by other stakeholders in the school

- High level of integrity

- Excellent time management and organisational skills

- Excellent communicator both oral and written

- Excellent attention to detail

Actuamos como agentes de cambio para construir una sociedad más justa y equitativa, donde la discapacidad esté plenamente integrada. Como parte de nuestra cultura People first, fomentamos organizaciones más humanas, donde cada persona pueda crecer y desarrollar su mejor versión.

Seas como seas y estés donde estés, en Eurofirms Foundation tienes un lugar para brillar.

Organización

Desde Eurofirms Foundation buscamos personas para incorporar en una beca de 12 meses en el área financiera de una empresa multinacional líder del sector farmacéutico, ubicada en Madrid.

Función

¿QUÉ VAS A APRENDER?

Finance Planning & Analysis (FP&A)

Estimación de ventas futuras mediante modelos predictivos por SKU.
Actualización de escenarios de planificación (LBE, PLAN, LRP).
Planificación de demanda por producto y análisis de inventario.
Análisis de situación macroeconómica.

Controlling & Master Data

Seguimiento de ventas por marca y control de procesos Order to Cash (OTC).
Soporte en reporting de cierre de ventas mensual.
Mantenimiento del maestro de materiales (creación, modificación, extensión…).

Business Partnering

Colaboración en el performance review de la afiliada (S&OP).
Preparación del forecast del equipo de ventas.
Análisis ad‑hoc de proyectos (promociones, lanzamientos, Blue Plans…).
Trabajo transversal para la estimación del volumen futuro de ventas.

Reporting

Análisis de desviaciones en líneas del P&L.
Reporting mensual de escenarios de planificación a stakeholders clave.
Requisitos
Grado en ADE, Finanzas, Economía, Matemáticas, Estadística, Ciencias Actuariales y Financieras, Contabilidad u otras titulaciones relacionadas.
Valoramos también candidaturas con formación universitaria en rama sanitaria.
Estar cursando un Máster en Ciencia de Datos, Big Data o relacionado con la formación cursada previamente que permita convenio de prácticas de 12 meses.
Inglés alto (C1).
Capacidad analítica.
Manejo avanzado de Excel, PowerPoint y conocimiento de Power BI.
Certificado de discapacidad igual o superior al 33% o IPT.

Convenio de prácticas de 12 meses, directamente con la empresa, con posibilidad de continuidad.

Descripcion_Completa:
Role:

Head of Construction – Owner’s Representative (Data Center)

Locations:

Madrid, Spain

Travel:

Yes — project-driven site presence required

Relocation:

Relocation assistance not specified

Experience Required:

Proven experience leading construction delivery of large-scale, mission-critical or data center facilities
Experience overseeing projects from pre-construction through commissioning and operational handover
Strong understanding of data center MEP systems, commissioning, and utilities coordination
Experience operating in a senior owner-side or consultancy-led delivery role
Professional fluency in Spanish and English

Certifications / Licenses (Required or Strongly Preferred):

Advanced project scheduling experience using P6 or equivalent
Experience with EPC/GC and multi-package delivery models

About Overwatch EMEA

Overwatch EMEA extends Overwatch Mission Critical’s proven delivery model across Europe, the Middle East, and Africa. We provide construction professional services, talent acquisition, and delivery leadership for mission-critical infrastructure projects, applying the same operational discipline, reliability, and veteran-led expertise that define our work in the United States.

From greenfield development to complex retrofits and live-site operations, our teams support the safe, efficient delivery of high-performance data center environments. At Overwatch, it’s more than a job, it’s a purpose.

Position Overview

As a Head of Construction, you will be part of a high-performing Overwatch EMEA team delivering large-scale, mission-critical data center developments in Spain.

Acting as an on-site representative of the owner, you will lead construction delivery from pre-construction through commissioning and operational handover. You will be responsible for safety, schedule, cost, quality, and risk across complex, fast-tracked capital programs while coordinating design, engineering, construction, utilities, and commissioning activities.

Responsibilities

Act as the end-to-end construction lead for mission-critical data center delivery programs.
Develop and execute construction delivery strategies aligned with project objectives and capacity requirements.
Partner with design and engineering teams to ensure scopes are coordinated prior to construction.
Lead pre-construction planning including constructability, logistics, utilities strategy, and site mobilisation.
Define and manage construction contracting strategies (EPC/GC, multi-package, early works).
Oversee all phases of data center construction, including base build, MEP, and commissioning readiness.
Manage EPC/GC partners, contractors, and vendors, driving accountability, safety, and performance.
Develop and manage construction budgets, forecasts, cost controls, and change management.
Identify delivery, schedule, and financial risks and implement mitigation strategies.
Conduct regular site engagement to validate safety, quality, coordination, and schedule adherence.
Maintain clear communication with stakeholders and support operational handover at project completion.
Implement HSE and quality assurance processes in compliance with applicable standards and regulations.

Qualifications

Proven experience leading construction delivery of large-scale, mission-critical facilities.
Strong understanding of data center construction, MEP systems, commissioning, and operational requirements.
Experience with EPC/GC delivery models, OFCI equipment, and utilities coordination.
Advanced project scheduling, tracking, and reporting capability (P6 or equivalent).
Strong budget management, forecasting, and commercial management experience.
Ability to operate autonomously in high-visibility, owner-side delivery roles.
Professional fluency in Spanish and English.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills with the ability to influence internal and external stakeholders.

Descripcion_Completa:
Segundo/a Jefe/a de Pizzería

¿Te apasiona la cocina italiana y el trabajo en equipo?

Buscamos un/a Segundo/a Jefe/a de Pizzería que quiera asumir un rol clave en la operación diaria, garantizando calidad, organización y excelencia en cada servicio.

Serás la mano derecha del Jefe de Pizzería, asegurando el correcto funcionamiento del área y liderando al equipo cuando sea necesario. Si disfrutas de los retos, la organización y la mejora continua, ¡queremos conocerte!

¿Cuál será tu misión?

Apoyar en la gestión operativa diaria de la cocina y la producción de pizzas, garantizando el cumplimiento de los estándares de calidad, higiene y eficiencia establecidos por la compañía.

Tus principales responsabilidades:

1. Gestión y liderazgo de equipo:

· Organizar y coordinar las tareas diarias, asegurando una distribución equilibrada del trabajo.

· Motivar y acompañar al equipo para alcanzar los objetivos del servicio.

· Asumir la responsabilidad del área en ausencia del Jefe de Pizzería.

2. Control de insumos y producción:

· Supervisar la calidad de las materias primas en la recepción.

· Realizar pedidos y asegurar una correcta recepción, almacenamiento y conservación.

· Ajustar la producción de masa e ingredientes según las necesidades del servicio.

3. Inventarios y control operativo:

· Realizar inventarios semanales y mensuales.

· Analizar rotación de productos y proponer mejoras en la gestión de insumos.

4. Supervisión de equipos:

· Detectar y comunicar oportunamente averías o necesidades de mantenimiento.

5. Coordinación interna:

· Informar al manager de sala sobre cambios en carta o disponibilidad.

· Colaborar estrechamente con cocina, sala, barra y gerencia para garantizar un servicio fluido.

Buscamos a alguien que:

· Tenga mínimo 3 años de experiencia previa en pizzería.

· Posea capacidad de liderazgo y organización.

· Trabaje con orientación a la calidad y atención al detalle.

· Sepa mantener la calma y eficiencia en momentos de alta demanda.

Si quieres formar parte de un equipo dinámico, en crecimiento y comprometido con la excelencia…¡Esta es tu oportunidad!

Descripcion_Completa:
¿QUIÉNES SOMOS?

En MP Software Técnico somos especialistas en soluciones de software CAD de alto rendimiento. Acompañamos a ingenieros y diseñadores para que saquen el máximo partido a sus herramientas de trabajo.

Como parte del equipo de Soporte Técnico Software CAD en MP Software Técnico, serás responsable de brindar soporte técnico a los usuarios, asegurando el correcto funcionamiento del software CAD. Tus tareas diarias incluirán la instalación del software, la asistencia en la resolución de problemas y la atención a consultas técnicas. Buscamos a alguien práctico que quiera un entorno estable y familiar.

Soporte Técnico: Ayuda directa al cliente en la instalación, configuración y activación de sus licencias.

Contacto Internacional: Comunicación con el fabricante (en inglés) para reportar errores o dudas técnicas.
Guías Rápidas: Creación de manuales sencillos y tutoriales para que los clientes aprendan a usar el software.

Experiencia: Al menos 2 años usando entornos CAD (ZWCAD, AutoCAD o similares).
Perfil Informático: Conocimientos básicos de redes y administración de Windows (permisos, instalaciones).

Air Liquide in Spain was founded in 1909 and today is made up of a dynamic team working in industry and healthcare throughout the country.



We offer gas, equipment and services for a variety of industries: from oil and gas, automotive, metal fabrication to chemical, pharmaceutical, agricultural, food and beverage and healthcare. Our local projects confirm the Group's commitment to developing innovative solutions that promote the energy transition, including hydrogen energy.



As a market leader, we aim to create value by offering cutting-edge solutions. We are committed to ensuring that our customers receive the best and most cost-effective performance from our products and services and that our patients receive the best quality of care. In today's competitive and ever-changing markets, it is the added value we offer our customers that makes the vital difference.




ES10061-ESPANA AL AIR LIQUIDE ESPAÑA, S.A.

Identificar y generar nuevas oportunidades de negocio en sectores clave como el tratamiento de aguas y efluentes, energía y movilidad con hidrógeno, utilidades industriales y la producción de plásticos y sus derivados, de forma que permita el cumplimiento de los objetivos marcados.

Gestionar todo el ciclo de cada proyecto, desde la prospección inicial hasta la firma del contrato.

Identificar socios estratégicos, construyendo y manteniendo relaciones sólidas.

Somos una empresa familiar, con un recorrido de más 25 años, con experiencia en todo tipo de instalaciones eléctricas, calefacción y climatización. Estamos ubicados en la zona sur de Madrid, y como empresa autorizada realizamos instalaciones, tanto a nivel particular como profesional. Nuestras premisas son la seriedad y la profesionalidad, nos gusta el trabajo bien hecho, ofreciendo una total garantía a nuestros clientes. Con el boom de las instalaciones fotovoltaicas y los puntos de recarga de vehículo eléctrico, nuestras líneas de negocio se han visto gratamente incrementadas por lo que afrontamos con mucha ilusión un futuro próximo muy provechoso en este aspecto. Además, ofrecemos un vehículo de empresa para desempeñar tus funciones y formación específica para el puesto, para que puedas desarrollar tus habilidades y competencias en el sector turístico.

Responsabilidades

Atender a los clientes: Recibir a los comensales con amabilidad y profesionalismo, proporcionándoles una experiencia inolvidable desde su llegada hasta su despedida.
Servicio de alimentos y bebidas: Utilizar correctamente la bandeja para servir los platos y bebidas, asegurando que la presentación y el servicio sean impecables.
Tomar pedidos: Escuchar atentamente las solicitudes de los clientes y asesorarles sobre las opciones del menú, incluyendo sugerencias de maridaje de bebidas.

Colaboración en equipo: Trabajar en estrecha colaboración con otros miembros del personal para garantizar una comunicación fluida y un servicio cohesionado.
Gestión de quejas: Manejar cualquier queja o inconveniente que pueda surgir, actuando con rapidez y profesionalismo para resolver la situación.

Requisitos

Experiencia comprobable: Mínimo 3 años de experiencia como camarero en entornos de restaurantes y/o hoteles de alta calidad.
Conocimientos en servicio al cliente: Habilidades excepcionales de atención al cliente con un enfoque en la satisfacción del cliente.
Habilidades de comunicación: Capacidad para comunicarse de manera clara y efectiva tanto con compañeros de trabajo como con clientes.
Trabajo en equipo: Demostrar un excelente desempeño en dinámicas de grupo, fomentando un ambiente de trabajo cooperativo y eficiente.
Resistencia y capacidad física: Capacidad para estar de pie durante largos períodos y realizar tareas físicas que incluyen levantar y transportar bandejas de alimentos y bebidas.
Flexibilidad horaria: Disponibilidad para trabajar en horarios variados, incluidos fines de semana y festivos, en función de las necesidades del negocio.

Ofrecemos

Contrato estable: Ofrecemos un entorno laboral seguro y profesional, con la posibilidad de crecimiento y desarrollo en el sector.
Formación continua: Oportunidades para participar en programas de formación y desarrollo profesional en el ámbito de la hostelería.
Ambiente de trabajo positivo: Integración en un equipo dinámico donde se valoran las aportaciones individuales y el trabajo colaborativo.

Si cumples con los requisitos y estás interesado en ser parte de un equipo comprometido con la excelencia en el servicio, te invitamos a postularte para el puesto de Camarero. Able2 es el proveedor total de ayudas técnicas para la casa y residencias.

Todos usan ayudas, jóvenes y mayores. Gafas para leer, un bastón para caminar, un abridor para abrir botes de verduras o un soporte para sostener. Todas son herramientas que hacen la vida más seguro y fácil.

Las herramientas de Able2 son accesibles, tienen buena calidad y tienen un precio razonable. Able2 solo entrega a través de distribuidores especializados; tiendas especializadas médicas, tiendas de atención domiciliaria, tiendas web, tiendas especializadas y de rehabilitación y farmacias. Hacemos entregas en todo España y Portugal.

Make an impact at NTT Global Data Centers

Join NTT Global Data Centers and be part of a team that drives innovation and sustainability in the digital world. With over 150 data centers across more than 20 countries globally, we offer unparalleled opportunities to work on cutting-edge technology and transformative projects. Experience a collaborative, innovative, and inclusive workplace where your ideas are valued, and your growth is supported.

Your role at a glance

As Senior Account Manager (m/f/d), you are part of our sales organization and will be in close contact with our existing and new clients.

What we are looking for

Key Roles and Responsibilities:

Qualify new leads and assess their potential
Develop and implement a strategy for spanish based clients to sell capacity of NTTs Madrid data center and further growth with the clients into other NTT data centers globally
Manage client and partner contracts, including the preparation of quotations, orders, and documentation
Analyze client / prospects and identify opportunities for new partnerships

- Develop a go-to-market (GTM) strategy in collaboration with Marketing, Product Management and Sales Strategy

- Develop a partner eco system in collaboration with the Partner Account Management

Analyze and process tenders, ensuring quality, accuracy, and procurement expertise
Conduct detailed analyses of requirements and derive necessary measures for customized solutions
Conceptualize solution approaches and strategies to meet specific requirements
Prepare offers and negotiate contracts for colocation services, including cross-selling and offer management for new and existing customers
Provide consultancy and customer support, particularly for services and network services
Manage commercial projects proactively and timely, ensuring accurate documentation and preparation of presentations for internal and external use
Communicate effectively across departments with internal and external contacts, including suppliers and external partners

Utilize the Group’s internal sales tools and methodologies to manage accounts, opportunities, pipelines, and forecasts effectively

Requirements:

Successfully completed commercial apprenticeship or comparable qualification
Multiple years of professional experience in the above field
Experience in the Data Center industry, ideally in a sales role for a Data Center operator in Spain
Strong executive presence and financial acumen
Ability to position colocation datacenter products against competitors
B2B selling expertise
Ability to drive beneficial revenue opportunities
Determine value, sales opportunities and strategies to maximize growth objectives
Understand and meet customer needs
Good MS Office skills and experience with CRM/ERP systems
Very good written and spoken English skills. Second language in german or spanish is a plus point


Soft skills

Independent and service-oriented way of working
Good communication and team-working skills
Strong written and verbal communication skills – candidate must be convincing and influential
Networking is key! Must be able to connect with customers and realize partnership opportunities
Work in geographically distributed and matrixed environments
Proactive and hands-on mentality

Who we are

As the third largest data center provider, we operate over 150 data centers in more than 20 countries and regions. We understand that every business – large and small – has its own unique needs and goals. We offer local-to-global data center expertise, aligned with our connected platform of AI-ready data centers to create solutions that enable our clients to seamlessly scale their digital businesses, anywhere and anytime.
